page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per core ratio. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, the instance utilizes an x86_64 architecture with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50th percentile. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and uncached memory read/write bandwidth, are positioned in the bottom 25th percentile. This performance profile indicates a tradeoff where the instance provides substantial memory capacity per core but exhibits constrained multi-threaded scaling and memory throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-4 offers a dense memory configuration relative to its vCPU count, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is appropriate for workloads such as static web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations or small-scale language model inference, where its average single-core performance and AMX-driven matrix capabilities can be leveraged without hitting multi-threaded bottlenecks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 105 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. The instance does not include local storage, GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU and text processing performance, but multi-core execution and memory bandwidth fall into the lower 25th percentile. This hardware profile offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive tasks that do not demand high multi-threaded throughput. It is suitable for static web serving, text processing, and small-scale model inference.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of system memory providing an 8 GB per core ratio
Dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Average single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Lower-tier multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth
